course_name: English for Customer Service & F&B (Intermediate B1)
course_description: Đón khách & đặt bàn, gọi món & gợi ý, dị ứng & chế độ ăn, bán lẻ, thanh toán, xử lý khiếu nại, câu bị động lịch sự, đặt bàn qua điện thoại, chỉ đường & gợi ý địa phương, trả lời review online.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Greeting & reservations
theory_pages.body:
### Greeting & reservations

*Good morning / Good evening. Welcome to [Restaurant name].*

- *Do you have a reservation?*  
- *Under what name, please?* (lịch sự hơn khi hỏi tên đặt bàn)

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Party size & fully booked
theory_pages.body:
### Party size & full house

- *How many people are in your party?* / *A table for how many?*  
- *A table for two, please.*

**Hết chỗ:** *I'm sorry, we are fully booked tonight. Would you like to wait at the bar?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Seating & menus
theory_pages.body:
### Seating & menus

- *Right this way, please.* / *Follow me, please.*  
- *Here are your menus. Your server will be with you shortly.*

content.reading_passage: Good evening. Welcome to Riverside Grill. Do you have a reservation? Under what name, please? How many people are in your party? Right this way. Here are your menus.

quizzes.question_text: Khách bước vào nhà hàng — lời chào chuyên nghiệp:
Hỏi khách đã đặt bàn chưa:
How many people are in your _____?
More professional when asking for the booking name:

options.B: What is your name? (same context as reservation desk)

quizzes.question_text: “Your server will be with you shortly” means the waiter is coming soon.
Guest: “We are three.” You respond professionally:

options.B: A table for three. Right this way.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Ordering flow
theory_pages.body:
### Drinks & ready to order

- *Can I get you something to drink to start off?*  
- *Are you ready to order?*  
- Guest: *We need a few more minutes.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Recommendations
theory_pages.body:
### Recommendations (upselling)

- *I highly recommend the grilled salmon.*  
- *Our chef's special today is …*  
- *Do you prefer meat or seafood?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Repeat & confirm
theory_pages.body:
### Confirming the order

- *Let me repeat your order. That's one steak, medium rare, and two Cokes.*  
- *Would you like anything else?*  
- *I'll have that right out for you.*

content.reading_passage: Can I get you something to drink? Are you ready to order? I highly recommend the salmon. Let me repeat your order: one steak, medium rare. Would you like anything else?

vocabulary.word: medium rare / chef's special / repeat the order
vocabulary.vi: chín tái / món đặc biệt / nhắc lại order

examples.vietnamese: Quý khách có dùng thêm gì không?

options.A: Can I get you something to drink?

quizzes.question_text: Guest can't decide yet. You say:
You should repeat the order back to avoid mistakes.
“Quý khách có dùng thêm gì không?”
Guest orders steak. You should ask:

options.A: How would you like your steak cooked?

quizzes.question_text: “I'll have that right out for you” means:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Allergies
theory_pages.body:
### Allergies

*Nuts / peanuts*, *seafood / shellfish*, *dairy*, *gluten*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Diets
theory_pages.body:
### Dietary preferences

*Vegetarian*, *vegan*, *halal*

*Does this dish contain meat?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Kitchen
theory_pages.body:
### Kitchen communication

- *I will check with the chef to be sure.*  
- *We can remove the peanuts for you.*

content.reading_passage: I'm vegan. Does this contain dairy? I will check with the chef. We can remove the nuts from the salad.

vocabulary.word: vegan / dairy / check with the chef
vocabulary.vi: thuần chay / sản phẩm sữa / hỏi bếp

examples.english: I will check with the chef.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi sẽ kiểm tra với bếp trưởng.

quizzes.question_text: “Tôi sẽ kiểm tra với bếp trưởng.”

options.A: I will check with the chef.
options.B: I will chef with the check.

quizzes.question_text: We can _____ the peanuts from the salad.
Guest has a severe allergy and asks about milk in soup. You're not sure. You:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Offering help
theory_pages.body:
### Greeting shoppers

- *Good afternoon. Are you looking for anything in particular?*  
- *Let me know if you need any help.*  
- Guest: *I'm just browsing / looking, thanks.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Sizes & fitting
theory_pages.body:
### Sizes, colors, fitting

- *What size are you?*  
- *We have this in blue and red.*  
- *The fitting rooms are right over there.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Stock
theory_pages.body:
### Stock

- *Let me check in the back for you.*  
- *I'm afraid we are out of stock in your size.*

content.reading_passage: Are you looking for anything in particular? I'm just browsing. The fitting rooms are over there. Let me check in the back.

vocabulary.word: browsing / in particular / out of stock
vocabulary.vi: chỉ xem / cụ thể / hết size-hàng

examples.english: Let me check in the back for you.
examples.vietnamese: Để tôi vào kho kiểm tra.

options.A: What do you want to buy?
options.B: Are you looking for anything in particular?

quizzes.question_text: The fitting _____ are right over there.
Fix: “I'm afraid we are out the stock.”
“Báo tôi nếu quý khách cần giúp đỡ.”

options.A: Let me know if you need any help.
options.B: Let me know you need if help.

quizzes.question_text: Guest asks for black — not on display. You say:

options.A: Let me check in the back.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Total & pay
theory_pages.body:
### Total & methods

- *Your total comes to $45.50.*  
- *How would you like to pay?* / *Cash or card?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Card & cash
theory_pages.body:
### Processing

- *Please insert or tap your card.*  
- *Please enter your PIN.*  
- *Here is your change.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Receipt & bag
theory_pages.body:
### Wrapping up

- *Would you like your receipt?*  
- *Do you need a bag for this?*  
- *Have a wonderful day. Hope to see you again!*

content.reading_passage: Your total comes to fifty dollars. How would you like to pay? Please tap your card. Here is your change. Would you like your receipt?

vocabulary.vi: tổng / mã PIN / hóa đơn / tiền thối

examples.vietnamese: Quý khách có lấy hóa đơn không?

quizzes.hint_text: How would you like to pay?

quizzes.question_text: Customer pays cash with extra — you return money and say:
PIN usually stands for Personal Identification Number.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Apologize
theory_pages.body:
### Acknowledge & apologize

Không đổ lỗi khách hoặc **đổ lỗi bếp trước mặt khách**.

- *I'm so sorry to hear that.*  
- *I apologize for the inconvenience.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Fix it
theory_pages.body:
### Solutions

- *I'll bring you a new one right away.*  
- *I'll send housekeeping up immediately.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Compensate
theory_pages.body:
### Compensate

- *Please let me offer you a free dessert.*  
- *I'll deduct this from your bill.*

content.reading_passage: I'm sorry the soup is cold. I'll bring a hot one right away. I apologize for the inconvenience.

vocabulary.word: de-escalation / inconvenience / deduct from the bill
vocabulary.vi: xoa dịu / bất tiện / khấu trừ vào hóa đơn

examples.english: I will bring you a new one right away.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi sẽ mang ra một phần mới ngay.

quizzes.question_text: Guest says the soup is cold. First line:

options.B: I apologize for that. Let me get you a hot one.

quizzes.question_text: Fix: “I will deduct this to your bill.”
You should blame the head chef in front of the guest.
quizzes.hint_text: Don't throw staff under the bus.

quizzes.question_text: “Tôi sẽ mang ra một phần mới ngay lập tức.”

options.A: I will bring you a new one right away.
options.B: I will right away new one bring.
options.C: I bring new one away right.

quizzes.question_text: Let me _____ you a free coffee.
Guest reports bad smell in room — best first step:

options.A: Apologize and offer room change / fix immediately

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Why passive
theory_pages.body:
### Why passive in service?

Khách quan, lịch sự — tránh công kích cá nhân.

- Blunt: *You didn't clean the room!*  
- Better: *The room has not been cleaned.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Structures
theory_pages.body:
### Be + past participle

- *Breakfast is served from 6 AM to 10 AM.*  
- *Your table is being prepared right now.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Fixed phrases
theory_pages.body:
### Common phrases

- *It is strictly prohibited.*  
- *The tax is included.*  
- *The tickets are sold out.*

content.reading_passage: Breakfast is served until 10. Your table is being prepared. Smoking is strictly prohibited. The tax is included.

vocabulary.word: is served / is included / sold out
vocabulary.vi: được phục vụ / đã gồm / bán hết

quizzes.question_text: Fix: “The tax is include in the price.”
“The glass was broken” is often more tactful than “You broke the glass”.
Instead of “You must pay tax”, softer:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Opening & details
theory_pages.body:
### Answering & details

*[Restaurant], Peter speaking. How may I assist you?*

- *For what date and time?*  
- *For how many people?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Spell & read back
theory_pages.body:
### Spelling & read-back

- *Could you spell your last name, please?*  
- *Let me read that back to you. 0-9-8-…*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Confirm
theory_pages.body:
### Confirm & close

- *So I have a reservation for Mr. Smith, a table for 4 on Friday at 7 PM. Is that correct?*  
- *We hold tables for 15 minutes.*  
- *We look forward to seeing you.*

content.reading_passage: Grand Cafe, how may I assist you? For what date and time? Could you spell your last name? Let me read that back to you.

vocabulary.word: assist / spell / read back / hold a table
vocabulary.vi: hỗ trợ / đánh vần / đọc lại / giữ bàn

examples.vietnamese: Tôi có thể giúp gì cho quý khách?

options.B: Grand Cafe, how may I assist you?

quizzes.question_text: More standard: “Let me read that back ___ you.”
Confirm all details before ending the call.
“Tôi có thể giúp gì cho quý khách?”
Restaurant is full that night. You say:

options.A: I'm sorry, we are fully booked that night.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Inside directions
theory_pages.body:
### Inside the building

*Restroom / toilets*

- *Go straight down the hall — it's on your right.*  
- *Take the elevator to the 3rd floor.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Transport
theory_pages.body:
### Transport help

- *Would you like me to call a taxi for you?*  
- *The nearest bus stop is a 5-minute walk from here.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Recommend & warn
theory_pages.body:
### Local tips

- *If you like history, you should definitely visit the Old Museum.*  
- *Just be careful with pickpockets in that area.*

content.reading_passage: The restrooms are down the hall. Would you like me to call a taxi? I highly recommend the old market — be careful with pickpockets.

vocabulary.vi: nhà vệ sinh / thang máy / móc túi

examples.english: Take the elevator to the third floor.

options.A: It's down the hall on your left.

quizzes.question_text: Would you like me to _____ a taxi for you?
The machine that moves between floors:
Fix: “Take the elevator at the 3rd floor.”
You may warn guests about pickpockets in busy areas.
“Trạm xe buýt cách đây 5 phút đi bộ.”

options.A: The bus stop is a 5-minute walk from here.

quizzes.question_text: Guest asks for local souvenirs. You:

options.A: I highly recommend the night market.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Positive replies
theory_pages.body:
### Positive reviews

- *Thank you for your kind words!*  
- *We are thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the food.*  
- *We look forward to welcoming you back soon.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Negative replies
theory_pages.body:
### Negative reviews

Structure: **Thanks for feedback → apologize → explain/fix → invite offline contact**

- *We sincerely apologize that your experience was not up to our usual standards.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Offline resolution
theory_pages.body:
### Take it offline

- *Please contact us directly at [email] so we can make this right.*  
- *We strive to provide excellent service and hope you give us another chance.*

content.reading_passage: Dear Anna, thank you for your kind words! We are thrilled you enjoyed the pasta. We look forward to welcoming you back. — Regarding your wait-time complaint: we sincerely apologize. Please contact us directly so we can make this right.

vocabulary.word: feedback / strive / up to our standards
vocabulary.vi: góp ý / nỗ lực / đạt chuẩn thông thường

examples.vietnamese: Xin vui lòng liên hệ trực tiếp với chúng tôi.

options.A: Thank you for your kind words!
We sincerely apologize for the delay.

quizzes.question_text: We are _____ to hear that you liked the service.
You should argue publicly with angry reviewers.
“Xin vui lòng liên hệ trực tiếp với chúng tôi.”
Your experience was not up to our usual _____.
Why give private email/phone for bad reviews?

options.A: Solve the problem privately and protect reputation
We look forward to welcoming you back.
